name: Tests clean up created records
instruction: Test files that create database records via payload.create must clean them up before the test exits, either inline or via afterEach/afterAll hooks.
relevant_when: TypeScript file under test/ with .spec.ts or .test.ts extension that calls payload.create to insert database records.
context: Tests that leak database records cause non-deterministic failures in other tests. Payload's test guidelines require all created records to be deleted before the test completes.
scope_reason: Each test file contains its own setup, teardown, and assertions.

checklist.name: cleanup-mechanism-exists
checklist.rule: The test file has a cleanup mechanism for records created via payload.create: either an afterEach/afterAll hook that deletes created records, or inline cleanup (payload.delete) after each test's assertions. Tracking IDs in a shared array with afterEach cleanup is the preferred pattern.

checklist.name: no-orphaned-creates
checklist.rule: Every payload.create call in a test body has a corresponding delete — either tracked in a shared ID array cleaned by afterEach, or deleted inline before the test function returns.
